prompt: My Jekyll travel blog has ~150 posts each with a 3MB hero photo. PageSpeed scores are terrible — LCP is 5+ seconds. I want responsive images with WebP fallback and lazy loading. I host on Netlify so I'm not stuck on the GH Pages allowlist. Walk me through the changes — Gemfile, _config.yml, layout changes, and the build pipeline.

assertions.text: Uses jekyll-picture-tag plugin (or equivalent responsive image solution)
Gemfile includes version pin for the plugin (e.g., ~> 2.1)
Configuration includes WebP format with fallback
Configuration includes multiple widths for srcset (at least 480, 800, 1200)
Includes lazy loading attribute in configuration
Notes about LCP optimization for above-the-fold images
Mentions Netlify build pipeline (no extra setup needed)
